Records.description: Three-loop banana in $D=2$ with three equal internal masses ($m_2=m_3=m_4$) and one distinct mass ($m_1$). The period vector is four-dimensional $(\Pi_2, \Pi_{12}, \Pi_{11}, \Pi_0)$. After a $GL(4,\mathbb{Z})$ change of basis the transcendental lattice becomes $T = H \oplus H(3)$. This matches families of K3 surfaces giving ordinary modular forms over $H \times H$, and the holomorphic period and mirror map admit an ordinary modular parametrisation (elliptic).
Records.familyName: three-loop banana (three equal masses) / three-loop banana with three equal masses (elliptic modular periods)
Records.method: K3 periods, transcendental lattice analysis, GL(4,Z) basis change, mirror map, modular parametrisation
Records.location: section 'Automorphic properties of three-loop banana integrals', subsection 'The three-equal-mass case m2=m3=m4' (bananas_2.tex)

Records.description: Three-loop banana integral in $d=2$ with four propagators (three mass $m_1$, one mass $m_2$). Two-parameter K3 surface. Holomorphic period factorizes as product of two copies of the two-loop equal-mass sunrise maximal cut. Modular parametrization via $\Gamma_0(6)$ and $\Gamma_0(12)$.

Three-loop banana integral family with two distinct internal masses in the 3-1 configuration: three propagators carry mass $m_1$ and one carries mass $m_2$. Nine denominators (four propagators + five ISPs). Computed in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. The underlying geometry is a K3 surface (Calabi-Yau 2-fold) with two moduli $z_1=m_1^2/p^2$, $z_2=m_2^2/p^2$. Seven master integrals: two tadpoles, four in the derivative block on the maximal cut, and one decoupling integral. The canonical differential equation is derived locally at the MUM-point $(z_1,z_2)=(0,0)$ with integer coefficients in the series expansion. This is the first epsilon-factorised form for this integral.

Three-loop banana family with three equal masses $m_1$ and one different mass $m_2$. 4 propagators + 5 ISPs; $D=2-2\varepsilon$; 7 masters via LiteRed2 IBP. Canonical epsilon-factorised DE; the K3 surface of the maximal cut factorises into a product of two elliptic curves, so periods and DE letters are expressed in meromorphic modular forms for $\Gamma_1(12)$. All master integrals in closed form as iterated integrals of modular forms.
Records.familyName: 3-loop banana with three equal masses and one different mass (4 propagators)
